required_authority_invariants: DEC-ERROR-INTERPRETER-001 — core/error_interpreter.py remains sole catalog authority; agent/error_handler.classify_error() continues to delegate to interpret()
DEC-ERROR-INTERPRETER-008 — universal-coverage contract: no Python traceback reaches the user without going through the interpreter (now extended to the chat-tool-dispatch surface)
DEC-AGENT-ERROR-HANDLER-001 — chat main-loop three-stage classify -> LLM-explain -> canned pipeline preserved at agent/chat.py call site; agent/error_handler.py BYTEWISE UNCHANGED

F62 — mode.run_fail remains sole authority for failure voice; consumed by render_interactive via _panel_title; the agent/tools.py:2136 string-concat prepend is REMOVED (folded into panel title; no duplication, no parallel authority)
F64 — panels are sole narration surface; LLM-facing strings carry no Rich markup; [USER_SAW_PANEL] + render_summary_line output are plain ASCII
Sacred Practice 5 (loud failure) — modules continue to call raise_for_status() raw; the boundary catches and translates

Sacred Practice 12 (single authority) — error translation lives ONLY in core/error_interpreter.py::_CATALOG; no parallel translator in agent/tools.py; no per-module mini-catalog (Option A per DEC-ERROR-ROUTING-001)

DEC-ERROR-ROUTING-002 supersession discipline — catalog extension is narrow, additive, named: HTTPStatusError 401/403 in _is_auth_error, 429 in _is_rate_limit, NEW _is_http_status_error_generic for 5xx/4xx fallthrough; ZERO rewrites or reorderings of existing entries (reviewer enforces via git diff)
DEC-ERROR-ROUTING-005 — every caught exception in execute_tool renders a panel (interactive=False); interpret() guarantees a non-None ErrorInterpretation for every exception

DEC-ERROR-ROUTING-006 — logger.exception at agent/tools.py:2130 downgraded to logger.debug(..., exc_info=True); traceback preserved in ~/.ap/debug.log via interpreter's _append_debug_log; ZERO user-visible stderr traceback
required_integration_points: src/adversary_pursuit/core/error_interpreter.py — ADD _is_httpx_http_status_error helper; EXTEND _is_auth_error (HTTPStatusError 401/403); EXTEND _is_rate_limit (HTTPStatusError 429); ADD _interpret_http_status_error_generic + final _CATALOG tuple (Service for 5xx, Network for 4xx fallthrough)

src/adversary_pursuit/agent/tools.py::ToolContext.__init__ — ADD console: Console | None = None kwarg + self.console = console or Console() line; ensure 'from rich.console import Console' import is present at module top

src/adversary_pursuit/agent/tools.py::execute_tool — REWRITE except Exception branch (~10 lines): downgrade logger.exception -> logger.debug(..., exc_info=True); call interpret() + render_interactive(interactive=False); return f'[USER_SAW_PANEL] {render_summary_line(interp)}' to LLM; remove _strip_rich_markup(ctx.mode_mgr.active.run_fail) prefix (folded into panel title via _panel_title)

tests/test_agent_tools.py — ADD 1 new test class TestExecuteToolFriendlyErrorRouting with 5 new test methods covering execute_tool exception-injection (401/429/500/connect-error/unknown)

forbidden_shortcuts: DO NOT edit agent/runner.py — BYTEWISE UNCHANGED (C-series + dispatch invariant); runner.py does not need to know about ctx.console

DO NOT edit agent/error_handler.py — chat main-loop pipeline already correctly wired via handle_error(); if a to_llm_summary() method on FriendlyError is tempting, RESIST — render_summary_line(interp) already provides the canonical LLM-facing string from the catalog side
DO NOT touch any of the 15 CTI/OSINT module files — Option A bound by DEC-ERROR-ROUTING-001; modules continue to raise loudly
DO NOT rewrite or reorder existing _CATALOG entries — additive only (DEC-ERROR-ROUTING-002); existing test bodies remain byte-identical
DO NOT introduce a new dataclass or sidecar (e.g., runner.last_error_panels) — DEC-ERROR-ROUTING-004 binds the ToolContext.console solution; sidecar would require runner.py edits

DO NOT add the [USER_SAW_PANEL] marker inside render_summary_line itself — that helper is shared with the smoke surface (which has no user-visible panel); the marker is added at the execute_tool call site ONLY
DO NOT remove the _strip_rich_markup helper — it is still used by cmd2 paths and by other helpers in tools.py
DO NOT change the 30 LLM tool dispatch entries (the long 'if tool_name == "..."' chain) — they are byte-identical; this slice only changes the except Exception branch at the end
DO NOT touch pyproject.toml, uv.lock, or any module under core/workspace.py, models/, dossier/, gamification/
DO NOT introduce a [y]/[n]/[d] interactive prompt inside execute_tool — interactive=False per DEC-ERROR-ROUTING-005 (mirrors cmd2 pexcept path)
DO NOT pre-stage MASTER_PLAN.md amendment in a separate commit — Phase 17O section + Plan Status row + Active Phase Pointer commit in the SAME implementer commit as source (AP #74 orphan-prevention)

DO NOT downgrade other logger.exception calls in tools.py (e.g., the ones in _workspace_summary, _search_workspace, _execute_* helpers) — universal coverage across THOSE surfaces is out of scope this slice (would be a follow-up hygiene slice)

DO NOT add catalog auto-fix entries for the new HTTPStatusError entries — DEC-ERROR-INTERPRETER-005 binds mechanically-safe-only (401 has no safe auto-fix; rate-limit's existing 429 auto-fix already covers retry_after when available)

DO NOT introduce an LLM system-prompt revision to acknowledge [USER_SAW_PANEL] — the marker is forward-compatible; the LLM produces a sensible terminal message either way; system-prompt revision is out of scope

DO NOT add the marker to the existing FriendlyError path (agent/error_handler.handle_error) — that path is the chat main-loop, separate from the tool-dispatch surface; mixing the marker conventions would create a parallel-mechanism trap

User-visible regression returns (raw traceback at chat prompt for HTTPStatusError 401), but no schema migration, no new external file, no env var to clean up. ~/.ap/debug.log is purely additive (delete-to-rollback if desired; entries are timestamped and harmless). Test count after revert: pre-slice baseline (the 10 new tests vanish). Rollback safety identical to Phase 10 universal interpreter's (pure-data + boundary-wiring revert with no side effects in workspace files, no migration state, no external system to reconcile).
